title: New automation condition: When column is empty

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
New automation condition: When column is empty

We’ve introduced a new condition in our automations, one that we think you’ll be very excited about.

Finally, you can set a condition to factor in whether a column is empty or not, enabling you to set reminders and notifications for situations where critical information is missing from columns

Whether you’re missing critical lead information or an email campaign asset - this new addition has got you covered

Feedback - we want to hear it!

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: This will eliminate so much chaos and extra automation runs. I’m so excited, thank you!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: We are so happy to hear this Soroh - thanks for sharing your feedback

posts.author: MichaelCrownSigns (Michael )
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely will use this for one of our boards. I’ll provide an update later on how beneficial it is.

posts.author: JHarrington (Jen H)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is it “live”? I am in desperate need of this for one of my teams and am not finding it under Automations.
Thanks!
Jen H

posts.author: RichT (Richard Turner)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is great news, will solve alot of issues we are having when the status colunms ate left blank, which we use for our shopfloor processes.

Is there a way we can get the automation to look at three status colunms and only send an notification if at least one is filled?

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: What you’re asking, which I would really want as well, is having “or” for the conditions rather than “and”. Such as if a is blank or b is blank or c is blank.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Jen - it’s live as a condition when building a custom automation. Let me know if you run into further issues here!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
I like this idea and hear where you’re coming from! At this time, if I am understanding correctly, you’d need to set up a few automations to cater for the different states of the status columns - i.e. when status 1 is empty but status 2 is not, etc…

Screen Shot 2023-11-03 at 9.21.50 am1072×868 39.3 KB

I understand this is a tedious process, so appreciate you raising this as feedback

posts.author: Mariolsen (Mari Olsen)
posts.cookedHtml: Amazing news - we love this! Is the plan to also roll this out for subitems too?

posts.author: ckornaros (Christos Kornaros)
posts.cookedHtml: @BiancaT Best news of the week ! Thanks a lot and add more and more functionalities on automation please

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Checking on this for you Mari - stay tuned!

Update: At this time, supporting subitems for this condition isn’t on out immediate roadmap, but I will do my best to advocate for this to be considered Mari!

Update: Check my latest reply for a new update - subitems will soon be supported

posts.author: giuseppe.amaranto (Giuseppe Amaranto)
posts.cookedHtml: It doesn’t work with Column File: so not so good for us

posts.author: Mariolsen (Mari Olsen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Bianca!

If anyone is in the same boat - For our use, we’ve set an automation to mark something as ‘doing’ if it’s completed without values in a number column. As a workaround for subitems we’ve had to set an initial automation that sets the time to 0, triggered by a column change that is mainly used at the start of any work to avoid it wiping anything throughout. Seems to work okay for now, but the ideal is of course the empty column automation condition

posts.author: Paul_Adams (Paul Adams)
posts.cookedHtml: Great idea, wouldnt it make sense that the “ghost” status is added to the column settings ie -“incomplete” (this wouldnt show as a selectable status option) so that shows as a ghost status in the automations? The columns would still look empty but for automation purposes would be handy?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Mariolsen - I have an exciting update! The developers have actually just confirmed that we can support subitems and will do so in the coming weeks - yay! Stay tuned!

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Soroh Donn reacted to your message:

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to have that. Now, I need to rename the “ghost” status in order to use it as a condition.

posts.author: Mariolsen (Mari Olsen)
posts.cookedHtml: Ahh, amazing news. Thanks Bianca!

posts.author: Joachim (Dessus)
posts.cookedHtml: looks promising! thanks

posts.author: SamiA-MB (Sami Aviles)
posts.cookedHtml: This is so wildly helpful and is going to solve for a handful of issues I’ve been running into lately cross-functionally.

posts.author: SamiA-MB (Sami Aviles)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bianca, I do have a question for you: I’m noticing that I can’t have this automation detect if a File column is empty, it seems? Why is that?

If you take a look at my screenshot here, our board has a “Final Script” column between “Messaging Points/Notes” and “Is Live Ad Read”, but it’s not coming up as an option to select.

Screenshot 2023-11-08 at 6.33.42 PM726×424 30.3 KB

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Sami, thanks for raising this. I have spoken with the team who clarified that the file column isn’t currently supported as an option for this condition just yet. The team are working to support all columns, however I am unable to confirm an eta just yet. I will keep you in the loop when more information is shared

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml: Amazing. This will definitely reduce a lot of hassle and will give more control over missed info scenarios. Thank you Monday Dev Team.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for your positive feedback Zaki! We will share this with our team internally

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

I wanted to let you know that the automation has been improved to included the following columns, previously unsupported:

  • File
  • World Clock
  • Location
  • Time tracking
  • Rating

We hope this is a welcomed update for you all

posts.author: Devin.T (Devin Tran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT,

When will subitems with a file column be supported?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Devin! We don’t currently have concrete plans to roll this out to subitems, that said, the team will monitor incoming requests and consider future developments. Thanks for your understanding

posts.author: marciosato (Marcio Yoshinori Sato)
posts.cookedHtml: supporting subitems, that’s i need to my board

posts.author: Mariolsen (Mari Olsen)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca! This is a change as early November this was coming within weeks. Is that no longer the case?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why not roll it out to subitems? Clearly its been possible to create subitem aware conditions because the subitem status columns are available as a conditional. It should be entirely possible to add the subitem columns to the bottom of the columns list in the empty conditional. Its possible to do it on the columns field already on the trigger.

That monday consistently chooses not to support subitems adequately across its platform while advancing flashy “AI” efforts is a slap in the face, and sends the message that only new features which can result in a press release to get picked up by blogs and tech outlets matter. Long term this kind of behavior will erode customer trust in monday.com - and in fact I believe has by sending the message that the overall health of the platform is less important than immediate stock price. Your paying customers should be driving the platform, not hedge fund managers.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @anon29275264, @marciosato and @Devin.T,

My apologies for the conflicting responses I provided regarding subitems! I did receive confirmation that this feature was going to be rolled out to subitems, which regretfully has recently has been redacted… I have followed up with the product manager in charge about this and will provide a confirmed answer as soon as possible. I appreciate your understanding and patience with me as I investigate

posts.author: mariag (Maria)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca! Do you know if the Recurring category will support the “column is empty” condition?

For example: I would like to have a weekly recurring automation to notify the person assigned to an item if a column is empty. I tried to create this using “every time period” but could not add the ‘column is empty’ condition to it.

Thank you!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Maria, thanks for your patience - I apologise for my late reply!

At this stage, I don’t believe we have concrete plans but if anything is made known to me I will certainly share the update here. Thanks for your understanding!

posts.author: klewis (Karli Lewis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is it possible to have an automation trigger when a column changes only if it was previously empty?
Ex: Move Item from Group A to B when column is filled in (changes from empty to not empty), but not if the data in the column simply changes.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: That would be the “if column is empty” condition here. Once its filled in, the automation would no longer trigger. The only exception would be if you then cleared the column using “clear column”

posts.author: frossini (Fab Rossini)
posts.cookedHtml: Can we have an update on when this will be available for subitems?

posts.author: frossini (Fab Rossini)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @BiancaT, Do you have any new about when this feature will be rolled out to subitems?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

Thank you for your patience with us

I’ve heard back from our product team who have communicated that we plan to support sub items in the condition by end of Q1 - your patience with us is always appreciated.

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Great, I’m so excited! It’ll be a real game changer for our company.

posts.author: Domandu (Dominick Coppinger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Following along for subitems supported as well, but have the same question as Karli.

Am trying to build an automation that will make a change only when data is first entered into a subitem column, not if the column is already populated and the data changes (is updated or removed).

I’ve got this working on item level columns, but whilst I can select subitem columns it doesn’t seem to work, so I’m hoping a Q1 release will solve this?

posts.author: donegal (donegal)
posts.cookedHtml: Does this work with the ‘Link’ column type?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @donegal - not at this stage I am afraid, however I will be sure to share this suggestion with the responsible team internally

posts.author: NEM (Chelley Andrews)
posts.cookedHtml: How do I create a condition where no one has been assigned?

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Use the workflows.

posts.author: NEM (Chelley Andrews)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am new to Monday and do not know where that it
I know automations but can’t figure out how to do it
